Home / Services / Subfloor Installation and Repair

Subfloor Installation and Repair

Ensure a solid foundation with our subfloor services.

A solid subfloor is essential for a long-lasting and beautiful floor. We specialize in subfloor installation and repair, ensuring a level and stable base for your new or existing flooring. Our experienced technicians can handle any subfloor issue, from water damage and rot to uneven surfaces and structural problems.

We understand the importance of proper subfloor preparation for a successful flooring installation. Our team will carefully assess your subfloor and recommend the best solution for your needs. Whether you need a complete subfloor replacement or a simple repair, we have the expertise to deliver exceptional results. We work with all types of subflooring materials, including plywood, OSB, and concrete.

We're committed to providing our customers with the highest quality subfloor services. Our goal is to ensure a solid foundation for your floors, protecting your investment for years to come.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Subfloor Inspection - Assessing the condition of your existing subfloor.
  2. 02 Step 2: Repair or Replacement - Repairing or replacing damaged sections of the subfloor.
  3. 03 Step 3: Leveling - Ensuring a level and even surface for the new flooring.
  4. 04 Step 4: Reinforcement - Strengthening the subfloor as needed to prevent future problems.

Benefits

  • Ensures a Solid Foundation
  • Prevents Flooring Problems
  • Extends the Life of Your Floors
  • Improves the Overall Appearance of Your Home

Frequently Asked Questions

Why is a good subfloor important?

A solid subfloor provides a stable and level surface for your flooring, preventing cracks, squeaks, and other problems.

How do I know if my subfloor needs repair?

Signs of subfloor problems include uneven floors, squeaking, soft spots, and water damage.

Can you install a new subfloor over an existing one?

In some cases, a new subfloor can be installed over an existing one, but it's important to assess the condition of the existing subfloor first.

Get a Free Estimate
